sections.paragraph: m. pl. 1Ki 10:22, and תּוּכִּיִּים 2Ch 9:21, according to Targ., Syr. (with the Arabic), Jerome and the Hebrews, peacocks. To this answer the Malabar. togeï, Sanscr. sikhi. From this domestic name of the bird comes also the Gr. ταώς, ταῶς, pr. ταϜῶς, Athen. ix. p. 397 (whence Arab. طاوس, Ch. טִיס), also pavo (t and p being interchanged; compare λαᾶς lapis, λίθος). See Bochart, Hieroz. tom. ii. p. 135, seqq.; and the late learned remarks of Ag. Benary in Annal. Litt. Berol. 1831, No. 96.
